data.title: Is There More Room for Amazon Stock (AMZN) to Run After Earnings?
data.description:
Amazon's (AMZN) stock has been on a bull run lately and is currently trading near its all-time high. The question now is: will the rally last after the company reports its financial results on April 29?

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Trade AMZN with leverage

In the past month, AMZN stock has risen nearly 30% as investors have moved back into growth stocks and the artificial intelligence (AI) trade. The shares are now trading at $260.02 each, near the all-time high for the stock of $261.03.

Amazon is seen as a big beneficiary of the AI boom as it develops its own data centers, microchips, and extends partnerships with leading artificial intelligence startup companies such as Anthropic. Analysts say Amazon will have to demonstrate continue growth in AI to keep its stock running higher after its upcoming earnings print.

Amazon's AI Moves

Amazon recently announced that it is investing another $25 billion in Anthropic. The deal will see Anthropic spend more than $100 billion on Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud computing technologies over the next 10 years.

Cloud computing remains an important metric in Amazon's quarterly financial results. Wall Street is always looking for continued growth in Amazon Web Services as it faces growing competition from established players such as Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOGL), as well as upstarts such as OpenAI.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 46 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 43 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $284.09 implies 14.91% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Spotify (SPOT) Q1 Earnings Review: Here's Why Stock Is Plunging despite Beating Estimates
data.description:
Spotify (SPOT) reported strong first-quarter results, marking a major milestone in the company's push toward higher margins and a more efficient cost structure. While the company beat Q1 earnings per share (EPS) and gross margin estimates, SPOT stock fell sharply, about 14% in regular trading, due to a weaker-than-expected revenue outlook for the second quarter.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Q1 revenue rose 14% year-over-year (constant currency basis) to €4.53 billion ($5.3 billion) and beat expectations of €4.5 billion. Furthermore, Spotify's adjusted earnings per share came in at €3.45 ($4.03), beating the consensus estimate of €2.95 per share and up 222.4% year-over-year. 

Record Profitability Drives a Strong Q1

Spotify posted record operating income and net profit, driven by higher subscription revenue, stronger ad monetization, lower content and operating costs, and continued margin gains across music and podcasts.

During the quarter, the company's user base continued to expand, with Monthly Active Users up 12% year-over-year to 761 million, beating guidance. Premium subscribers grew 9% to 293 million, roughly in line with expectations, and the platform added 10 million total users in Q1.

Outlook Missed the Mark

Despite strong results, Spotify's outlook came in below Wall Street estimates. Softer guidance for revenue and operating income signaled a slower path to profitability than investors hoped. Management cited higher planned spending on product and AI features, a more cautious view on ad growth, and timing effects in subscriber additions.

The company guided to €630 million in operating income for the current quarter, below the €680 million that analysts were expecting. Also, Spotify expects to add six million premium subscribers this quarter, reaching 299 million, just shy of Wall Street's 300 million forecast.

The shortfall raises concerns about consumers' willingness to pay more in a tough economy, especially after Spotify raised the U.S. individual premium price to $12.99 from $11.99 in February.

Is SPOT a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, SPOT has a Strong Buy consensus based on 16 Buy and four Hold ratings assigned in the past three months. SPOT stock's consensus price target is $635.53, implying a 48.75% upside.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 4/28/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading as AI jitters return to the market following a projection miss from OpenAI.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

According to the Wall Street Journal, OpenAI missed projections for revenue and weekly users, with CFO Sarah Friar warning that funding for future computing power could be at risk if revenue doesn't grow fast enough. AI stocks, including Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), and Oracle (ORCL), are trading lower on the report.

U.S.-Iran Talks Stall as Trump Weighs Next Steps

Elsewhere, negotiations between the U.S. and Iran remain stalled, with President Trump leaving the option of resuming attacks against Iran on the table. In addition, Trump is reportedly displeased with Iran's proposition of ending the war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the U.S. lifting its blockade on Iranian ports. He's expected to provide a counteroffer in the coming days.

In addition, Trump claimed that Iran is in a "state of collapse" and wants Hormuz reopened "as soon as possible." Oil prices continue to rise as the waterway faces severe traffic restrictions.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: OpenAI Misses Revenue Targets as the IPO Clock Keeps Ticking
data.description:
OpenAI has reportedly fallen short of its recent targets for both user growth and revenue. According to The Wall Street Journal, the company missed several monthly revenue goals earlier this year as it lost momentum to Anthropic in coding tools and enterprise markets. This has raised concerns among some executives, especially as OpenAI pushes forward with its aggressive IPO timeline. Notably, OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman plans to take the company public by late 2026.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

OpenAI Falls Short of 1B ChatGPT Users

The report said ChatGPT's growth slowed toward the end of last year, and OpenAI missed its internal goal of reaching 1 billion weekly active users by year-end. The company also fell short of its annual revenue target for ChatGPT and has reportedly been facing higher subscriber losses, adding more pressure to its growth story.

OpenAI's Chief Financial Officer, Sarah Friar, has reportedly warned company leaders that future computing contracts could become harder to fund if revenue growth does not improve. This has raised concerns about whether the company can continue supporting its massive spending on data centers and AI infrastructure.

The growing pressure is also forcing closer attention on costs ahead of a possible IPO. While Altman has been pushing big expansion plans, Friar and other executives are now focusing more on financial discipline and tighter spending. Earlier this month, Friar reportedly flagged potential risks around the company's readiness and its huge financial commitments ahead of IPO.

However, Altman and Friar denied those reports, saying that any suggestion of disagreement between them or any pullback in expanding computing resources is "ridiculous."

OpenAI Faces Intense Competition

OpenAI is also facing rising competition as Google's (GOOGL) Gemini gained strong momentum late last year, cutting into its market share. At the same time, Anthropic has been putting pressure on OpenAI in coding tools and enterprise markets, where businesses are increasingly looking for reliable AI solutions.

Anthropic has been gaining attention with its Claude models, especially among developers and large companies that need stronger coding support and safer AI systems. This growing competition has made it harder for OpenAI to maintain its early lead in the fast-moving AI market. As rivals continue to expand, investors are watching closely to see whether OpenAI can speed up user growth and defend its position ahead of a potential IPO.

Although OpenAI remains a private company, investors can still monitor its performance and key developments through TipRanks' Private Companies Center. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Galaxy Digital Abandons Crypto Chaos to Build a 1.6GW AI Super-Grid as Q1 Losses Shrink
data.description:
Galaxy Digital (GLXY) narrowed its Q1 losses on Tuesday, as the firm moved away from the big swings of the digital market. The company is now focusing on the massive demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure. This shift is already showing results as the firm hands over its first major data center space to a big partner.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade CRWV

Galaxy Digital Delivers its First AI Data Center Hall

The company just finished the first part of its Helios campus in Texas and handed it to the AI firm CoreWeave (CRWV). This is a big win because it starts a long-term lease that brings in steady money. While revenue dropped to $10.2 billion compared to last year, the net loss of $216 million was actually better than what experts predicted.

The firm says that "disciplined expense management during the quarter helped narrow the adjusted EBITDA loss," proving they are spending their cash more wisely.

Helios Doubles its Power to Feed the AI Market

To stay ahead of the competition, the company secured approval to double the power capacity at its Texas site. The facility will now have more than 1.6 gigawatts of power, which is enough to run some of the largest AI systems in the world.

By the end of June, the site will already provide 133 megawatts of computing power. This move turns the company into a major owner of the physical systems that make AI possible.

How the AI Shift Will Impact GLXY Stock

This shift into AI data centers helps GLXY stock because it makes the company less dependent on unpredictable crypto prices.

  • Reliable Income: Long-term leases with AI firms like CoreWeave mean Galaxy knows exactly how much money is coming in every month.
  • Massive Scale: Having 1.6 gigawatts of power approved makes Galaxy one of the biggest players in the infrastructure space.
  • Efficiency: Cutting costs while building out these centers shows investors that the leadership is focused on long-term profit.

Is Galaxy Digital a Good Stock to Buy?

Galaxy Digital stock carries a Strong Buy consensus, based on 13 Wall Street analyst ratings over the past three months. Out of those, 11 analysts call it a Buy, two rate it a Hold, and none suggest a Sell.

The average 12-month GLXY price target stands at $38.85, which implies nearly a 53.1% upside from the last price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Is Down Today and Why One Investor Is Finally Buying In
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) shares slipped about 3% today after a report that OpenAI fell short of its own user growth and revenue targets, raising questions about how sustainable near-term AI demand might be. The news rattled the broader chip space, with investors focusing on the possibility that slower expansion at one of the most important AI customers could ripple through massive computing agreements involving Nvidia and its peers.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

So, is Nvidia's strong growth trajectory now in danger? Its fiscal fourth-quarter results suggest otherwise, with revenue reaching $68.1 billion, up 73% year over year, alongside margins of 75%.

For investor Andres Veurink, the risk that hyperscaler capital spending would eventually normalize and weigh on future growth was enough to keep him on the sidelines. His view, however, has actually started to shift.

"My concerns are still there," Veurink noted, "but NVDA seems to be able to tackle this, and the way their product portfolio is developing, I think they can rely on even more revenue streams going forward."

While Nvidia's GPUs are the company's central revenue generator, it is the growing networking and software segments that have moved the needle for Veurink. The investor makes special mention of Nvidia's Spectrum-X software, the company's Ethernet platform that boosts AI efficiency and performance.

This is further entrenching Nvidia's hold on the chips market, argues Veurink. The investor points out that Nvidia's numerous software services make the choice to remain with Nvidia an obvious one.

"It makes little sense to use a different network platform than what the maker of the chips is providing," Veurink added.

As for the potential market, Veurink notes that it's no longer just the hyperscalers who are building data centers, as sovereign players have entered the scene. That eases his fears of customer concentration, and the investor calls governments one of the "most important growth areas" for the company.

"I was still on the fence about [how] to rate the stock," Veurink concluded, "but I think I'm going to bite the bullet and rate it a Buy." (To watch Veurink's track record, click here)

Wall Street analysts, however, don't need to bite any bullets to get there, with NVDA carrying a Strong Buy consensus rating. The average 12-month price target stands at $274.38, pointing to 29.5% upside from current levels. (See NVDA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Citi Says Anthropic, Not OpenAI, Will Lead $4.2 Trillion AI Market Boom by 2030
data.description:
Citigroup Inc. (C) has raised its long-term outlook for the global artificial intelligence (AI) market. The bank says enterprise demand for AI tools is moving faster than expected, especially for coding and automation. As a result, Citi now projects that the AI market could explode to $4.2 trillion by 2030. The bank points to Anthropic, the AI firm behind the Claude chatbot, as the key player that could drive this growth, placing it ahead of other AI rivals like OpenAI, Google (GOOGL), and DeepSeek.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Citi Lifts AI Market Forecast with Anthropic Driving Growth

After watching how quickly companies have begun using AI systems, Citigroup raised its global market forecast for the AI industry. The bank said in an April 27 note that it expects the global AI market to explode above $4.2 trillion by 2030, reflecting a massive jump from its earlier estimate of just over $3.5 trillion.

Much of that growth is expected to come from a surge in enterprise AI demand. Citi believes this part of the market will generate about $1.9 trillion in less than five years, up from an earlier forecast of $1.2 trillion. The bank credits the rise to stronger demand for AI tools that help big firms code more easily, automate workloads, and boost system productivity.

Within this new outlook, Citi places Anthropic as a leading force set to drive global enterprise adoption. The bank stated that the company's massive revenue and demand growth are driven by its Claude AI models and Claude Code tools. It also pointed to Anthropic's Mythos AI preview, noting that rather than short-term monetization, this new model could bring greater benefits in the future.

Citi also noted that Anthropic is mainly focused on enterprise users and had prioritized this market early on. This strategy had given the firm a major edge in commercial rollouts, with over 80% of its massive revenue now generated from enterprise customers. As such, the bank has named Anthropic "the leader in enterprise AI," placing it above OpenAI, one of its biggest AI rivals.

Anthropic Enterprise Growth Challenges OpenAI and Global Rivals

Citi's report also shows how quickly Anthropic has scaled its global operations. The bank noted that the company's annual revenue run rate (ARR) had climbed past $30 billion by April, marking one of the fastest growth phases in the sector. Most of that revenue came from enterprise clients, showing that Anthropic is focused more on selling to businesses than to everyday consumers.

Another reason Citi sees Anthropic driving the global AI market's value is its recent infrastructure deals. The company has partnered with Google in a deal worth up to $40 billion and also signed a $125 billion agreement with Amazon (AMZN) this week. These contracts show the massive computing power required to support Anthropic's growth and model development.

At the same time, competition across the AI industry is rising. OpenAI, Google, and other major players are also pushing further into enterprise markets. The focus is no longer just on which model performs best. Reliability, real-world integration, and workflow efficiency now matter just as much. Citi suggests that this shifting competitive landscape will shape the next phase of the AI market, as firms battle for dominance in the business space.

Is Citi a Good Investment?

The Citigroup (C) stock currently carries a Strong Buy consensus rating from 14 Wall Street analysts tracked by TipRanks. Out of them, 13 analysts recommend a Buy, 1 assigns a Hold, and 0 suggest a Sell. The stock also has a 12-month price target of $142.9, implying about 10.9% upside from current levels. Additional analysis on the stock is available via the TipRanks Stock Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

With its share price down 10% this year, Microsoft has been the laggard among the Magnificent 7 mega-cap technology stocks. As such, analysts and investors are looking for the company to turn things around when it reports what will be its fiscal third-quarter 2026 earnings.

data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump Says Iran Wants Hormuz Reopened 'As Soon as Possible'
data.description:
President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Iran told the U.S. it wants to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as soon as possible. The waterway has remained at a near standstill since the beginning of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28, creating a global oil shock.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

"Iran has just informed us that they are in a 'State of Collapse.' They want us to 'Open the Hormuz Strait,' as soon as possible, as they try to figure out their leadership situation (Which I believe they will be able to do!)" Trump said in a Truth Social post.

U.S.-Iran Talks Stall as Oil Pressure Mounts

Earlier this week, Trump claimed that Iran has only three days left until it is forced to pause oil production, which could lead to permanent infrastructure damage. The U.S. naval blockade on Iranian ports has disrupted the country's ability to export oil.

The two countries remain deadlocked in negotiations. Iran previously offered an end to the war and the reopening of Hormuz in exchange for the U.S. lifting its blockade. However, Trump was reportedly unhappy with the proposal and is expected to provide a counteroffer in the coming days.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Grok Predicts an XRP Price of '$500+ by 2035' as Trader Unmasks Ripple's 10-Year Masterplan
data.description:
Popular trader Vincent Van Code shared an update last week regarding the future of XRP (XRP-USD). Based on a deep study using the AI tool Grok, he suggested that the price could soar to "$500+ by 2035." While he admits this is not a guaranteed result, the analysis highlights a massive shift in how the market views the long-term value of the coin.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Vincent Van Code Runs a Deep AI Study

The trader spent many hours using an LLM to look into every part of the ecosystem. This study looked at variables for the next 10 years, including emerging trends in AI, micropayments, and neo-banks. He described the process as iterative and focused on objective data. He also mentioned that after doing this work, he now sees the "long-term, 4D chess strategy" that the team behind the coin is playing.

The XRP Price Target Depends on Several Events

The huge price target depends on several major events happening exactly as expected. The analysis assumes that the CLARITY ACT passes and that U.S. policies on digital assets stay positive. It also factors in future company acquisitions and the growth of decentralized finance (DeFi) outside of traditional banks. Vincent Van Code noted that these numbers are based on "things going to plan" over the next decade.

Quantum Proofing Secures XRP's Network

One of the most specific parts of the 10-year outlook involves a major technical update. The study expects a successful deployment of quantum-proofing with an estimated arrival in 2028. This would protect the system against future computer threats and keep it ahead of the competition. The trader called the team "truly geniuses" for thinking so far ahead with their technical roadmap.

Even with such a high price target, Vincent Van Code told his followers to stay grounded. He explained that these numbers are for entertainment and come from an AI analysis, not just his personal opinion. He warned everyone to "take this with a grain of salt" and made it clear that this is not financial advice. He also told people to avoid leverage trading and to always do their own research because the market can still be very risky.

At the time of writing, XRP's price is sitting at $1.3760.
data.author: Annika Masrani
